L1.area: Cryptographic hash computation backend with hardware-accelerated SIMD instruction support

L2.description: A cryptographic weakness exists in the SHA-2 hash implementation where an accelerated computation backend incorrectly loads message data when processing multiple blocks simultaneously. This causes the hash function to produce incorrect digest values for messages that span more than one processing block, undermining the integrity guarantees of the hash output.

L3.area: SHA-512 hardware-accelerated SIMD computation backend for x86 architecture
L3.description: A cryptographic weakness exists in the SHA-512 AVX2-accelerated backend where incorrect memory offsets are used when loading message data for dual-block processing. This causes the hash function to silently produce incorrect digest values for messages longer than a single SHA block (128 bytes), undermining the integrity and security guarantees of SHA-512 hashes.

ground_truth.reason: The AVX2 backend for SHA-512 uses incorrect data pointer offsets and swapped lane insertion order when loading message blocks, causing it to miscompute hash digests for messages spanning multiple SHA blocks.
